title: Two-expert lighting dispatcher with from-scratch trunks and factorized row/column coordinate fields
hypothesis: Ten straight reverted rounds (36-45) all left the joint 32x32=1024-way single-softmax cell classifier untouched -- margin losses (34/36) and trunk-objective swaps (41-44) fought over the SAME one-shot 1024-way decision, coordinate reparameterizations (39/40) still asked one softmax to pick 1 of 1024 joint hypotheses, multi-hypothesis regression (37) and retrieve-then-rerank (45) both tried to patch the same decision from outside without changing its shape. Per champion-failure-mode-probes memory, that decision is the actual wall: the true cell is the field's top-1 pick only ~2% of the time even though it lands in the model's own top-20 (of 1024) 33-58% of the time -- the head is starved of clean per-class signal because 1024 mutually confusable classes each get only ~1/1024 of the gradient budget, and lookalike cells split that budget further. This round is also forced to abandon the mnv3s_features8.pt / mobilenet_v3_small trunk that every kept experiment since #11 (12-45) has carried unexamined, so the trunk swap is mandatory rather than optional -- rather than treat that as a tax to pay while leaving the field mechanism alone (which is exactly the failure pattern the patience rule is calling out), this design uses the forced trunk change as the occasion to factorize the coordinate decision itself: instead of one 1024-way joint classifier, use two INDEPENDENT 32-way classifiers, one over row-bands (v-axis) and one over column-bands (u-axis). A row-band and column-band decision each get ~32x the effective per-class training exposure a joint cell gets, and most confusable lookalike districts share one axis (same latitude band, different longitude, or vice versa) but rarely both -- so decomposing the decision directly attacks the top-1-vs-top-20 gap instead of reparameterizing around it again. Combined with this is a genuine (not merely nominal) attempt at the unified-vs-dispatcher axis CLAUDE.md SS3 flags as never resolved: two fully independent from-scratch trunks (bright-scene and low-light experts, no shared weights, no pretrained init) blended by a cheap raw-pixel-statistics dispatcher, rather than the single-shared-trunk + gated-head-blend compromise tried in exp 12/30 (both of which kept the SAME shared trunk and were never a real dispatcher test).
method: model/model.py: delete the ImageNet MobileNetV3-Small trunk entirely (_build_pretrained_trunk, PRETRAINED_TRUNK_PATH, IMAGENET_MEAN/STD, the torchvision import) and the joint 1024-way loc_logits/dark_logits/layout_squeeze field. Build two independent from-scratch conv trunks (BrightTrunk, DarkTrunk -- same 4-layer depthwise-separable architecture, separately initialized, no weight sharing, no pretrained loading), each mapping 128x128x3 -> ~64x8x8. A lightweight dispatcher (2-layer MLP over 3 cheap raw-pixel statistics: mean luminance, luminance std, dark-pixel fraction -- no trunk dependency) outputs one sigmoid gate weight. Each trunk's 8x8 feature map is axis-pooled (mean over width -> row descriptor, mean over height -> column descriptor) into two small linear heads producing 32-way row-band and 32-way column-band logits; the two experts' row logits and column logits are each blended by the dispatcher gate. Row and column probabilities are independently 1-D soft-argmaxed into v and u (replacing the old 2-D soft-argmax over a joint field). The C4 exact-rotation inference-time ensemble (4x forward passes) is removed -- rotation robustness moves to training time instead (model/train.py: every epoch, each sampled location is rendered at all 4 exact 90-degree headings instead of one random continuous heading, using 1/4 as many distinct locations per epoch to hold the total crop/step budget fixed), which also frees the compute the two-trunk design spends (net: 2 trunks x 1 pass replaces 1 trunk x 4 passes -- roughly half the old inference compute). Confidence head reads row/col marginal peak mass + entropy plus a new signal only possible with genuinely independent experts: the distance between the bright-only decode and the dark-only decode (detached, high disagreement = low confidence). train.py's optimizer drops the pretrained-trunk 10x-lower-LR split (nothing is pretrained anymore -- all params train at one LR) and its init-strategy log string is updated to from-scratch per SS9.
expected_outcome: Primary lever is the factorized field, not the forced trunk swap (which we expect roughly metric-neutral on its own since it only restores from-scratch capacity comparable to what exp 41-44's from-scratch attempts already showed is viable). If the top-1-vs-top-20 gap is really a per-class starvation problem, decomposing 1024 joint classes into 2x32 independent-axis classes should measurably lift top-1 rate and pull the worst-case median error below the 743.07 m plateau -- targeting roughly 450-620 m. A null result (no material change or a regression) would argue the ranking wall is not primarily a training-signal-density problem, which would itself be informative after 10 failed attempts.
eli5: Instead of one trunk trying to guess 1-of-1024 map squares in a single shot -- a task made brutally hard because dozens of squares just look alike -- we split the question in two much easier ones asked at the same time: 'which north-south band is this?' and 'which east-west band is this?' (32 choices each, instead of 1024 combined). We also build two completely separate small vision experts from scratch -- one tuned for bright scenes, one for dark ones -- with a cheap brightness-o-meter deciding how much to trust each, instead of one borrowed off-the-shelf vision network doing everything. And rather than showing the model four rotated copies of every photo at flight time (slow), we show it all four rotations during training instead, so it learns to handle any camera heading on its own without paying that cost when it's actually flying.

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128x128 px crop, one of 6 lighting renders

stages.name: Lighting dispatcher
stages.detail: cheap raw-pixel brightness/contrast statistics (mean luminance, luminance std, dark-pixel fraction) through a tiny 2-layer MLP, no trunk dependency, output one sigmoid blend weight between a bright-scene expert and a low-light expert

stages.name: Feature extractor
stages.detail: two independent from-scratch conv trunks (bright-scene expert, low-light expert), 4 depthwise-separable conv layers each, stride 16 down to 64ch x 8x8, no shared weights, no pretrained init

stages.name: Coordinate field
stages.detail: each expert's 8x8 feature map is axis-pooled into an 8-bin row descriptor and an 8-bin column descriptor; a small linear head per axis per expert produces 32-way row-band and 32-way column-band logits (replaces the old single 1024-way joint cell classifier); the two experts' row and column logits are blended by the dispatcher gate

stages.name: Coordinate decode
stages.detail: row and column probability distributions are each independently soft-argmaxed (1-D expected value) into v and u, instead of one 2-D soft-argmax over a joint 1024-cell field

stages.name: Confidence head
stages.detail: row/col marginal peak mass and entropy, plus the disagreement distance between the bright-only decode and the dark-only decode (a signal only possible with two truly independent experts), feed a calibrated hit predictor

stages.name: Output (lat, lon, confidence)
stages.detail: frozen export contract: 1x3x128x128 float32 in, [u, v, conf] out

stages.name: Rotation-robust training coverage
stages.detail: each epoch trains every sampled location at all 4 exact 90-degree headings (not one random continuous heading), using 1/4 as many distinct locations to hold the crop budget fixed -- replaces the removed inference-time 4-way rotation ensemble

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: row-marginal cross-entropy + column-marginal cross-entropy (each against a 1-D Gaussian-smoothed target) + expected-coordinate L2 + confidence BCE; gradient into each expert weighted by the same dispatcher gate used at inference

implementation_brief: model/model.py (rewrite; keep module docstring history, append a new dated entry rather than deleting prior ones; keep GRID_K=32, TARGET_SIGMA_CELLS=1.5, DECODE_BETA=3.0, GOOD_ERR_UV=0.05 as-is): (1) Delete `from torchvision.models import mobilenet_v3_small`, `_build_pretrained_trunk`, `PRETRAINED_TRUNK_PATH`, `IMAGENET_MEAN`, `IMAGENET_STD`, and the `norm_mean`/`norm_std` buffers -- no pretrained weight file may be loaded or referenced anywhere in model/. (2) Add a `SmallTrunk(nn.Module)` class: stem Conv2d(3,32,3,stride=2,padding=1)+BN+ReLU (128->64), then 3 depthwise-separable blocks (depthwise 3x3 stride 2 + pointwise 1x1, each +BN+ReLU) taking channels 32->48->96->128, spatial 64->32->16->8; forward returns the final [N,128,8,8] map. Size each expert so the two trunks combined stay well under half the old single 1024-way FC's 2.2 MB (i.e. keep total exported ONNX <= 4 MiB with margin -- check `export_onnx` output size after implementing and shrink channel widths uniformly if over budget; do not change grid_k or drop a stage to make budget). (3) In `TinyLocNet.__init__`, replace `self.features` with `self.bright_trunk = SmallTrunk()` and `self.dark_trunk = SmallTrunk()` (separate instances, independent parameters, no `.load_state_dict` anywhere). Add `self.dispatch = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(3, 16), nn.ReLU(), nn.Linear(16, 1))` (raw-pixel-stat gate). For each trunk add two linear heads: `row_head_bright/col_head_bright` and `row_head_dark/col_head_dark`, each `nn.Linear(C*8, GRID_K)` where C is the trunk's output channel count (128 above) -- input is the flattened axis-pooled descriptor (`fmap.mean(dim=3).flatten(1)` for row, `fmap.mean(dim=2).flatten(1)` for col). Confidence head input dim grows by 1 (add the cross-expert disagreement scalar) -- keep it a small 2-layer MLP as before. Remove `layout_squeeze`, `loc_logits`, `dark_logits`, `gate`, `_c4_stack`, and the old flat length-1024 `cell_u`/`cell_v` buffers. Replace them with a single length-32 `axis_centers` buffer (`(torch.arange(GRID_K)+0.5)/GRID_K`, the same values `_grid_centers` used per row/column before flattening to 1024) reused for both the row decode (against v) and the column decode (against u). (4) `forward(self, x, return_logits=False)`: compute raw-pixel stats directly from `x` (mean, std, and `(x.mean(dim=1) < 0.1).float().mean(dim=(1,2))` for dark-pixel fraction) -> gate `g = torch.sigmoid(self.dispatch(stats))`; run `fmap_b = self.bright_trunk(x)`, `fmap_d = self.dark_trunk(x)` (no C4 replication, single pass, batch size N throughout); compute row/col logits per expert, blend: `row_logits = (1-g)*row_bright + g*row_dark`, same for col; sharpened decode `p_row = softmax(DECODE_BETA*row_logits)`, `p_col = softmax(DECODE_BETA*col_logits)`, `v = (p_row*axis_centers).sum(1)`, `u = (p_col*axis_centers).sum(1)` (the single length-32 `axis_centers` buffer from step 3, reused for both axes); unsharpened `p_row1`,`p_col1` (temperature 1) for the same peak/entropy/gap confidence features as before, computed per axis and combined (e.g. sum or mean of the two axes' peak mass and entropy). Additionally compute `decode_bright` and `decode_dark` (each axis's blend-free 1-D soft-argmax using only that expert's own row/col logits, sharpened, detached) and feed `disagree = sqrt((u_bright-u_dark)^2 + (v_bright-v_dark)^2 + eps)` into the confidence head. `conf = sigmoid(conf_head(...) - conf_shift)`. `out = cat([u, v, conf], dim=1)`; when `return_logits=True` return `(out, row_logits, col_logits)` (three values, not two -- update every call site). Default branch (export path) still returns exactly `out`, a [N,3] tensor -- do not change this, `export_onnx`/the ONNX contract depend on it. (5) `loss_fn(pred, row_logits, col_logits, target_uv)`: build 1-D Gaussian-smoothed targets independently per axis (`g_row = softmax-normalized exp(-((axis_centers - target_v)*32)^2/(2*1.5^2))`, same for col against target_u), CE each against `log_softmax(row_logits)`/`log_softmax(col_logits)`, sum the two CEs; keep the existing coordinate L2 (`((pred[:,:2]-target_uv)**2).sum(1).mean()`) and confidence BCE terms unchanged in form. model/train.py: update every `model(xb, return_logits=True)` call site to unpack 3 values and pass both logits tensors into `loss_fn`; delete the `trunk_params = list(model.features.parameters())` / two-LR-group optimizer split (nothing is pretrained, so use one `torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=1e-3)` group, still wrapped in the existing `CosineAnnealingLR` schedule over the same `total_steps`); in `sample_epoch`, change the per-crop heading draw from one `angle = rng.uniform(0,360)` per picked location to iterating `for heading in (0, 90, 180, 270)` per picked location (same realization image reused across all 4 headings of a given location), and reduce the per-bucket location draw to `min(max_crops_per_bucket, len(crops)) // 4` distinct locations so total crops/epoch (and thus `steps_per_epoch`/`total_steps`) stays unchanged -- `extract_crop`'s exact-multiple-of-90 angle argument must still go through the frozen `pipeline.dataset.extract_crop` unchanged, just called 4x per location instead of once; update `info["init"]` in `train_area` to `"from-scratch: two independent conv trunks (bright/dark experts), no pretrained weights"` per SS9. Frozen contracts to restate and preserve exactly: train.py's CLI (`python -m model.train --area ... --out-dir ... --epochs ...`) and model.py's ONNX export contract (1x3x128x128 float32 [0,1] input, `[[u, v, conf]]` output, opset 17, dynamo=False) — pipeline/score.py depends on both and must not need any change.
